info.title: Hexalith Timesheets Capture Contracts
info.summary: Stable contract description for Timesheets time capture, Timesheet Period submission, Activity Type catalog, evidence reads, and metadata descriptors.

nonOwnedState: Project or Work names, hierarchy, planning, or lifecycle state

retentionCategories.name: TimeEntryEvidence
retentionCategories.guidance: Time Entry evidence is retained by default.

retentionCategories.name: ExportRecord
retentionCategories.guidance: Export record retention requires tenant policy.

retentionCategories.name: MagicLinkConfirmationAuditMetadata
retentionCategories.guidance: Confirmation audit metadata retention is unresolved.

commentRules: Comments may be excluded by policy.
Comment export is disabled until policy allows it.

SubmitTimeEntriesForApproval.description: Submit one or more Draft Time Entries for approval. Tenant, user, correlation, role, and authorization context are server-derived and not accepted from the caller payload.

SubmitExternalTimeEntry.description: Submit external contributor time through the same Timesheets capture flow. Tenant, actor, correlation, authority, workflow policy, and message envelope fields are server-derived and not accepted from the caller payload.

ConfirmExternalTimeEntry.description: Record external contributor confirmation evidence for an existing Time Entry. Confirmation is not approval and carries no approval authority.

IssueMagicLinkConfirmationCapability.description: Issue one scoped, single-use magic-link confirmation capability. Tenant, issuer, authority, correlation, and hash material are server-derived and not accepted from the caller payload.

RevokeMagicLinkConfirmationCapability.description: Revoke one previously issued magic-link confirmation capability. Tenant, actor, and correlation fields are server-derived.

ExpireMagicLinkConfirmationCapability.description: Record policy-driven expiry for one magic-link confirmation capability. Tenant, actor, and correlation fields are server-derived.

ConfirmTimeThroughMagicLink.description: Confirm scoped time after server-side capability validation. The payload carries no authority or attribution fields; the server derives all attribution from the validated capability.

AdjustTimeThroughMagicLink.description: Adjust policy-allowed proposed-entry fields after server-side capability validation. Caller payload is restricted to editable proposed-entry values.

MagicLinkIssueResponse.description: One-time issuance response that may contain the usable opaque token. This schema is not persisted, projected, exported, or exposed by metadata/read models.

SubmitTimesheetPeriod.description: Submit one weekly or monthly Timesheet Period. Tenant, submitter, submitted timestamp, tenant time-zone policy, correlation, role, and authorization context are server-derived and not accepted from the caller payload.

ApproveTimesheetPeriod.description: Approve one Submitted Timesheet Period. Tenant, user, correlation, authority source, approver, decision timestamp, and authorization context are server-derived and not accepted from the caller payload.

RejectTimesheetPeriod.description: Reject one Submitted Timesheet Period or selected included entries with required reasons. Tenant, user, correlation, authority source, approver, decision timestamp, and authorization context are server-derived and not accepted from the caller payload.

ApproveTimeEntry.description: Approve one Submitted Time Entry. Tenant, user, correlation, role, authority source, approver, and authorization context are server-derived and not accepted from the caller payload.

RejectTimeEntry.description: Reject one Submitted Time Entry with a required reason. Tenant, user, correlation, role, authority source, approver, and authorization context are server-derived and not accepted from the caller payload.

CorrectRejectedTimeEntry.description: Correct one Rejected Time Entry by submitting additive correction values. Tenant, user, correlation, authority source, actor, and authorization context are server-derived and not accepted from the caller payload.

CorrectApprovedTimeEntry.description: Add a correction to one Approved Time Entry through additive correction values and a required correction reason. Tenant, user, correlation, authority source, actor, approval lineage, and authorization context are server-derived and not accepted from the caller payload.

TimeEntrySubmitted.description: Domain event emitted for one accepted Time Entry submission. It records server-derived submitter and tenant evidence without exposing raw envelopes or command bodies.

TimeEntryContributorConfirmed.description: Domain event for external contributor confirmation evidence. It records confirmation evidence only and does not approve, lock, or make a Time Entry ledger-eligible.

TimesheetPeriodSubmitted.description: Domain event emitted for one accepted Timesheet Period submission. It records server-derived tenant, submitter, UTC audit instant, tenant-local boundary evidence, included Time Entry IDs, and period state without exposing raw envelopes or command bodies.

TimesheetPeriodApproved.description: Domain event emitted for one accepted Timesheet Period approval. It records grouped period decision evidence and included Time Entry IDs without exposing raw envelopes or command bodies.

TimesheetPeriodRejected.description: Domain event emitted for one accepted Timesheet Period rejection. It records grouped period evidence, affected Time Entry IDs, selected-entry reasons, and authority attribution without exposing raw envelopes or command bodies.

TimeEntryApproved.description: Domain event emitted for one accepted Time Entry approval. It records approver, tenant, UTC decision time, approval decision id, resulting state, authority source, and approval scope without exposing raw envelopes or command bodies.

TimeEntryRejected.description: Domain event emitted for one accepted Time Entry rejection. It preserves the affected entry id, approver, tenant, UTC decision time, required reason, approval decision id, resulting state, authority source, and approval scope without exposing raw envelopes or command bodies.

TimeEntryCorrected.description: Domain event emitted for one accepted rejected-entry correction. It links prior and corrected values to the rejection decision while preserving server-derived actor and tenant evidence.

TimeEntryApprovedCorrected.description: Domain event emitted for one accepted approved-entry correction. It preserves previous values, corrected values, actor, UTC timestamp, reason, source approval decision lineage, resulting approval state, and resulting correction state without editing approved evidence in place.

AiEffortMetrics.description: AI effort evidence stored separately from authoritative human/external duration. Units are explicit and provider token metrics remain nullable when unavailable or not reported.

wallClockDurationMilliseconds.description: AI wall-clock execution time in milliseconds.

modelRuntimeMilliseconds.description: AI model or tool runtime in milliseconds.

billableEffortMinutes.description: AI billable effort in whole minutes. This does not reinterpret durationMinutes and does not convert tokens or runtime into human hours.

providerInputTokenCount.description: Provider-reported input token count. Provider token counts are nullable when not reported or unavailable; zero means the provider explicitly reported zero.

providerOutputTokenCount.description: Provider-reported output token count. Provider token counts are nullable when not reported or unavailable; zero means the provider explicitly reported zero.

providerTotalTokenCount.description: Provider-reported total token count. Provider token counts are nullable when not reported or unavailable; zero means the provider explicitly reported zero.

AiEffortMetricSourceMetadata.description: Compact AI metric source metadata for audit. Does not carry prompts, responses, secrets, bearer tokens, raw request or response bodies, caller authority, or Party personal data.

providerName.description: Generic provider identifier when provider-originated metrics are reported.

toolName.description: Generic tool identifier when tool-originated runtime metrics are reported.

workExecutionId.description: Stable work execution context identifier when supplied by the caller's work orchestration.

ActivityTypeCatalogCommand.description: Create, rename, update metadata, deactivate, reactivate, or restrict Activity Types at tenant or project scope. Project-scoped commands carry only a stable Project reference.

ActivityTypeCatalogReadModel.description: Tenant Activity Type catalog read model with stable IDs, active/inactive text status, capture-selection availability metadata, and projection freshness.

TimeEntryEvidenceReadModel.description: Evidence read model with stable references, source authority, safe event lineage, read-time display hydration state, approval/correction placeholders, direct-edit lock evidence, optional AI metrics, optional policy-aware comment metadata, and projection freshness metadata.

TimeEntryLockEvidence.description: Safe direct-edit lock evidence for display surfaces. Approved entries are locked from direct edits. This carries stable approval decision, scope, approver reference, and UTC lock time when available without caller authority or upstream payload details.

TimeEntryCorrectionValues.description: Snapshot of entry facts before or after a correction. Display labels and sibling-owned personal data are hydrated at read time.

TimeEntryCorrectionEvidence.description: Correction evidence that links original values, corrected values, actor, tenant, UTC correction time, and rejection decision lineage.

TimeEntryApprovedCorrectionEvidence.description: Approved correction evidence that links original values, corrected effective values, actor, tenant, UTC correction time, correction reason, and source approval decision lineage.

TimeEntryApprovalDecisionEvidence.description: Stable approval or rejection evidence for a Time Entry. It uses stable Party/Tenant references, authority attribution, scope, UTC decision time, and optional rejection reason without copying display names or raw authority payloads.

TimesheetPeriodSelectedEntryRejectionEvi.description: Selected-entry rejection evidence supplied as business intent and later stored with grouped period rejection evidence. It contains only the affected Time Entry ID and required entry rejection reason.

TimesheetPeriodApprovalDecisionEvidence.description: Stable approval or rejection evidence for a Timesheet Period. It preserves period state separately from entry states and includes affected entry ids, authority attribution, UTC decision time, and optional rejection reasons.

TimeEntryEventLineageItem.description: Safe domain event summary for evidence lineage. It omits raw envelopes, stream names, message identifiers, tenant/user context, and payload bodies.

TimeEntryDisplayHydration.description: Read-time display labels supplied by owning sources when available. Durable Timesheets evidence remains stable references only.

TimeEntryHydratedDisplayLabel.description: Optional read-time label with explicit fresh, stale, unavailable, denied, or unknown state.

TimesheetPeriodSummaryReadModel.description: My Timesheet Period read model with period state separate from entry Approval State, tenant-local boundary evidence, included entries, blocking guidance, entry summaries, and projection freshness.

ApprovalAuthoritySourceAttribution.description: Stable approval authority evidence for later approval decisions. It records the action, source, decision state, policy key, policyVersion, and freshness without accepting caller authority input.

ExternalContributionSource.description: Safe external source metadata for idempotency and audit. It must not contain personal data, raw provider payloads, bearer credentials, capability payload material, display names, comments, or upstream problem details.

MagicLinkConfirmationScope.description: Safe magic-link target scope. It carries stable IDs only and no display names, comments, authority decisions, capability secrets, or caller-supplied tenant fields.

MagicLinkAuditMetadata.description: Correlation-safe audit source metadata. It must not contain capability secrets, capability payload material, comments, personal data, display names, or command bodies.

MagicLinkConfirmationCapabilityIssued.description: Auditable issuance event. Stores hash material and safe metadata only; capability secrets and capability payload material are excluded.

MagicLinkConfirmationCapabilityUsed.description: Auditable single-use event for a magic-link confirmation capability. Stores scoped identifiers and safe source metadata only.

MagicLinkConfirmationCapabilityReadModel.description: Operator-visible magic-link state. Excludes capability secrets, capability payload material, hashes, narrative text, display names, and command bodies.

MagicLinkAdjustmentDisplayResponse.description: Safe external adjustment display details returned only after capability validation. Read-only and editable fields are explicit.

MagicLinkConfirmationDisplayResponse.description: Safe external confirmation display details returned only after capability validation. Excludes authority, approval, and sibling-owned state.

TimeEntryContributorConfirmationEvidence.description: Contributor confirmation evidence exposed to authorized approvers. It is separate from approval evidence.

TimesheetsMetadataDescriptor.description: Runtime-neutral metadata for generated command forms, projection views, fields, actions, and state badges.

AiTokenMetricAvailability.description: AiTokenMetricAvailability distinguishes provider-reported token counts from token metrics that were not reported or are unavailable.

post.summary: Confirm scoped proposed time through a magic link
Adjust scoped proposed time through a magic link
